A night-shift network engineer monitors an organization's infrastructure, which consists of a series of interconnected servers. The reliability of each server $ S_i $ in the network can be modeled by a continuous random variable $ R_i $ following a normal distribution $ N(\mu_i, \sigma_i^2) $. The engineer also collaborates with the cybersecurity team to mitigate potential threats, which can be modeled as a Poisson process with a rate $ \lambda $ of attacks per hour.

1. Given that the network consists of 10 servers, each with a reliability $ R_i $ where $ \mu_i = 0.98 $ and $ \sigma_i = 0.02 $, calculate the probability that at least 9 out of the 10 servers will have a reliability of at least 0.95 at any given time.

<<The probability that at least 9 out of the 10 servers will have a reliability of at least 0.95 at any given time is calculated using the binomial distribution. Each server has a probability of 0.98 of having a reliability of at least 0.95, and the probability of failure is 0.02. The probability of at least 9 successes in 10 trials is the sum of the probabilities of 9 and 10 successes. This is calculated as follows:>>
